frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Yaar, I get that safety is a big deal, but this constant 'all men are creeps' narrative just isn't fair. My husband, my male colleagues – they're all genuinely good guys. It's so frustrating when women generalize and make every man feel like a potential threat. Not all men are like that, why can't we acknowledge that instead of always assuming the worst?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment, from a woman, expresses frustration at women's generalizations about men, using the 'Not all men' dismissal. It personalizes the issue by referencing her husband and colleagues as 'good guys' and challenges the rationality of women's wariness, suggesting women 'always assum[e] the worst' rather than acknowledging nuances.
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 38. Married. A working woman in Bengaluru. You found out last week that your husband has been texting another woman from his office. He says it is nothing serious and has promised to stop. You are on a long phone call with your sister. You have decided to stay in the marriage. But in describing what happened you are directing almost all your anger at the other woman and almost none at your husband. You are telling your sister what kind of woman she must be to text a married man and what women like her want.
